figures.caption: Table 3: Adaptability performance on Criteo. Improvement percentages reflect the gains achieved by applying our framework to the backbone models. Bold face indicates the best results.

Figure 1: Overview of Light-FMP. The framework consists of three phases. Pretraining: A DRS model MS (the basic architecture is shown on the left) is augmented with a masking layer and trained on a small subset DS , yielding the pretrained model MT and a learned mask. Pruning: The mask prunes both feature embeddings and MT , producing selected features and model MP . Continued training: MP is trained on the remaining dataset D −DS using only pruned features, producing the final model MO .
Figure 2: Experimental results on Criteo using xDeepFM.

Table 2: Overall experimental results of feature selection using xDeepFM backbone. The pruning ratio for all methods except No Selection and MvFS is set to 0.5. Time values are represented as ratios relative to the original training time: 15,803 seconds for Criteo and 6,323 seconds for Movielens and 15,312 seconds for Avazu. Bold face indicates the best results of each column. The results are an average of 3 random runs.
